# Awesome-VibeCoder

> A production-grade toolbox of AI agent definitions, modular system instructions, reusable skills, and multi-agent SDLC workflows for AI coding tools (Claude Code, opencode, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, Antigravity).

Full catalog of every agent, skill, instruction, and workflow in this repository, one entry per line, grouped by asset class. See AGENTS.md for the cross-host entry point and docs/opencode.json for a ready-to-paste opencode instantiation.

## Agents (agents/*.agent.md)

- [agents/Beast.agent.md](agents/Beast.agent.md): Autonomous coding agent that maps context first, then executes end-to-end with high precision.
- [agents/CoderBeast.agent.md](agents/CoderBeast.agent.md): Autonomous prompt-driven coding agent: sanitizes the given prompt, refreshes technical path context when needed, executes the task end-to-end, then maintains the prompt and implementation document in one continuous run.
- [agents/ExpertCoder.agent.md](agents/ExpertCoder.agent.md): Hybrid prompt-driven agent: sanitizes the given prompt, refreshes technical path context when needed, generates a deterministic implementation plan, executes with structure, then maintains the prompt and implementation document.
- [agents/PromptFileAuthor.agent.md](agents/PromptFileAuthor.agent.md): Prompt-only agent: creates, rewrites, sanitizes, and maintains .prompt.md files through the prompt-authoring skill chain. Does not implement product code.
- [agents/RepositoryPathAuditor.agent.md](agents/RepositoryPathAuditor.agent.md): Executes repository technical path audits by loading the local technical-path-indexer skill, consuming its structured index output, and rendering evidence-backed freshness checks or canonical technical paths markdown.
- [agents/agent-governance-reviewer.agent.md](agents/agent-governance-reviewer.agent.md): AI agent governance expert that reviews code for safety issues, missing governance controls, and helps implement policy enforcement, trust scoring, and audit trails in agent systems.
- [agents/context-researcher.agent.md](agents/context-researcher.agent.md): Deep-dive research agent that investigates code, bugs, GitHub issues, or any topic across the repository and produces an evidence-backed research file pinpointing exact files, lines, and root causes before implementation begins.
- [agents/electron-angular-native.agent.md](agents/electron-angular-native.agent.md): Code Review Mode tailored for Electron apps with Node.js main processes, Angular renderers, and native integration layers.
- [agents/expert-react-frontend-engineer.agent.md](agents/expert-react-frontend-engineer.agent.md): Expert React 19.2 frontend engineer specializing in modern hooks, Server Components, Actions, TypeScript, and performance optimization
- [agents/gpt-5-beast-mode.agent.md](agents/gpt-5-beast-mode.agent.md): Beast Mode 2.0: A powerful autonomous agent tuned specifically for GPT-5 that can solve complex problems by using tools, conducting research, and iterating until the problem is fully resolved.
- [agents/modernization.agent.md](agents/modernization.agent.md): Human-in-the-loop modernization assistant for analyzing, documenting, and planning complete project modernization with architectural recommendations.
- [agents/planning-agent.agent.md](agents/planning-agent.agent.md): Produces architecture advice, formal specifications, and deterministic implementation plans for humans or AI without executing product code.
- [agents/refine-issue.agent.md](agents/refine-issue.agent.md): Refine product requirements and GitHub issues by adding Acceptance Criteria, Technical Considerations, Edge Cases, and NFRs.
- [agents/scientific-paper-research.agent.md](agents/scientific-paper-research.agent.md): Research agent that searches scientific literature and retrieves structured experimental data using the BGPT MCP server.
- [agents/sdlc-accessibility-specialist.agent.md](agents/sdlc-accessibility-specialist.agent.md): WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA audits, accessibility-first design review, structured remediation reports, and a11y requirements ownership — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-api-designer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-api-designer.agent.md): API-first design, OpenAPI 3.1 contracts, REST/GraphQL/gRPC style guidance, and api-contracts.md ownership — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-backend-engineer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-backend-engineer.agent.md): API design, service implementation, data access, integration patterns, and observability — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-code-reviewer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-code-reviewer.agent.md): Structured code review against SOLID, security, performance, and architecture standards — emits findings to .sdlc/decisions/review-*.md, never edits product code
- [agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect.agent.md](agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect.agent.md): Threat modeling (STRIDE/DREAD), security architecture, Zero Trust, and compliance frameworks — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer.agent.md): Secure coding, OWASP vulnerability remediation, security testing, and scanner configuration — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-db-architect.agent.md](agents/sdlc-db-architect.agent.md): Data modeling, schema design, normalization, indexing strategy, and migration planning — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-db-developer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-db-developer.agent.md): Query optimization, stored procedures, migration scripts, and database performance tuning — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-developer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-developer.agent.md): General-purpose software implementation with clean code, SOLID principles, and production-grade quality — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-devops-engineer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-devops-engineer.agent.md): CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, containerization, monitoring, and deployment strategies — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-frontend-engineer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-frontend-engineer.agent.md): Senior frontend engineering — UI components, state management, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), and performance — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-fullstack-engineer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-fullstack-engineer.agent.md): End-to-end feature implementation spanning frontend and backend — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md](agents/sdlc-orchestrator.agent.md): Optional coordinator that initializes .sdlc/ workspace, decomposes goals into tasks, dispatches to role agents, monitors progress, and advances SDLC phases
- [agents/sdlc-product-manager.agent.md](agents/sdlc-product-manager.agent.md): Requirements analysis, user stories, issue management, prioritization, and roadmap planning — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-qa-tester.agent.md](agents/sdlc-qa-tester.agent.md): Test strategy, test automation, quality gates, coverage analysis, and quality reporting — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-responsible-ai.agent.md](agents/sdlc-responsible-ai.agent.md): Bias prevention, accessibility compliance, ethical AI, privacy-by-design, and inclusive system review — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-scrum-master.agent.md](agents/sdlc-scrum-master.agent.md): Agile coaching, sprint planning, ceremony facilitation, impediment removal, and continuous improvement — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-software-architect.agent.md](agents/sdlc-software-architect.agent.md): System design, technology selection, component boundaries, scalability patterns, and ADRs — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-technical-writer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-technical-writer.agent.md): Developer docs, API reference, architecture docs, tutorials, and user guides — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/sdlc-ux-ui-designer.agent.md](agents/sdlc-ux-ui-designer.agent.md): JTBD analysis, user journey mapping, flow specifications, design system guidance, and accessibility requirements — works standalone or as part of an SDLC team
- [agents/search-ai-optimization-expert.agent.md](agents/search-ai-optimization-expert.agent.md): Expert guidance for modern search optimization: SEO, Answer Engine Optimization (AEO), and Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) with AI-ready content strategies.
- [agents/simple-app-idea-generator.agent.md](agents/simple-app-idea-generator.agent.md): Brainstorm and develop new application ideas through fun, interactive questioning until ready for specification creation.
- [agents/web-design-system-engineer.agent.md](agents/web-design-system-engineer.agent.md): Owns design tokens, component primitives, theming, and accessibility primitives across the frontend — bridges design and engineering.
- [agents/web-performance-engineer.agent.md](agents/web-performance-engineer.agent.md): Defines and enforces Core Web Vitals and asset budgets; profiles and remediates frontend performance regressions.

## Skills (skills/<name>/SKILL.md)

- [skills/ai-prompt-engineering-safety-review/SKILL.md](skills/ai-prompt-engineering-safety-review/SKILL.md): Comprehensive AI prompt engineering safety review and improvement prompt. Analyzes prompts for safety, bias, security vulnerabilities, and effectiveness while providing detailed improvement recommendations with extensive frameworks, testing methodologies, and educational content.
- [skills/ai-workflow-architect/SKILL.md](skills/ai-workflow-architect/SKILL.md): Ensure clear boundaries between Agents and Skills, enforce semantic naming conventions, clarify objectives, prevent logic loops, eliminate redundancy, and build highly reliable, stateless AI workflows. This skill is critical for designing and auditing AI Agents and Skills to ensure they are deterministic, maintainable, and free of logic errors.
- [skills/api-contract-first/SKILL.md](skills/api-contract-first/SKILL.md): OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI, JSON-Schema patterns, deprecation, idempotency-key, problem+json, cursor pagination — the canonical API contract design skill consumed by API Designer and implemented against by Backend Engineer.
- [skills/architecture-option-evaluator/SKILL.md](skills/architecture-option-evaluator/SKILL.md): Evaluate architecture options, boundaries, and consolidation decisions for agents, skills, modules, or workflows. Use when the user asks to merge overlapping roles, compare structures, reduce redundancy, or choose an implementation architecture. Produces a current-state summary, explicit options, a recommendation, trade-offs, and migration risks.
- [skills/context-map/SKILL.md](skills/context-map/SKILL.md): Generate a map of all files relevant to a task before making changes
- [skills/context-researcher/SKILL.md](skills/context-researcher/SKILL.md): Investigate code, bugs, GitHub issues, error traces, or any topic the user names, gather evidence across the repository (exact files, line ranges, git history, related tests, dependencies, prior art), and produce a structured research file that pinpoints root causes, where changes must go, and open risks. Use when the user asks to research, investigate, analyze, root-cause, or "look into" something before writing code, or explicitly requests a research/investigation report.
- [skills/css-architecture/SKILL.md](skills/css-architecture/SKILL.md): ITCSS/CUBE/BEM CSS layering, cascade layers (@layer), CSS Modules vs. CSS-in-JS trade-offs, and container queries — the canonical CSS-at-scale skill consumed by Frontend Engineer and Web Design System Engineer.
- [skills/formal-specification-writer/SKILL.md](skills/formal-specification-writer/SKILL.md): Write machine-readable specifications for features, process changes, agent behavior, and workflow definitions. Use when the user asks for a formal spec, explicit requirements, acceptance criteria, constraints, or durable planning artifacts. Produces structured markdown with purpose, scope, definitions, REQ/CON/SEC/PAT items, interfaces, and acceptance criteria.
- [skills/implementation-plan-generator/SKILL.md](skills/implementation-plan-generator/SKILL.md): Generate deterministic implementation plans that are executable by humans or AI. Use when the user asks for step-by-step planning, task decomposition, merge sequencing, refactor planning, or AI-ready execution plans. Produces phased tasks, identifiers, dependencies, file impact, validation steps, and rollback-aware risk notes.
- [skills/observability-three-pillars/SKILL.md](skills/observability-three-pillars/SKILL.md): OTLP/JSON structured logs, RED/USE metrics, distributed tracing (W3C Trace Context), SLO/SLI/error-budget, RUM vs LAB telemetry — the canonical observability skill consumed by DevOps Engineer and Backend Engineer.
- [skills/planning-quality-gate/SKILL.md](skills/planning-quality-gate/SKILL.md): Validate draft architecture advice, implementation plans, and specifications for ambiguity, overlap, missing dependencies, unverifiable steps, and weak output contracts. Use immediately before finalizing a planning deliverable. Produces pass-fail findings and concrete corrections required for release quality planning artifacts.
- [skills/premium-frontend-ui/SKILL.md](skills/premium-frontend-ui/SKILL.md): A comprehensive guide for GitHub Copilot to craft immersive, high-performance web experiences with advanced motion, typography, and architectural craftsmanship.
- [skills/prompt-builder/SKILL.md](skills/prompt-builder/SKILL.md): Create production-ready GitHub Copilot .prompt.md files by running a structured discovery interview (purpose, persona, task, context variables, tools, output, validation) and then generating the complete prompt file content. Use when the user needs a new prompt file or wants a full rewrite of an existing one. Composable with prompt-maintainer (post-run improvement), prompt-markdown-sanitizer (sanitize output), and technical-overview-maintainer (document implementation changes). Supports MCP tool interface for pipeline integration.
- [skills/prompt-eval-and-regression/SKILL.md](skills/prompt-eval-and-regression/SKILL.md): Golden outputs, model-graded evals, scorer matrix (deterministic, heuristic, LLM-as-judge, human), CI integration with pass/fail thresholds — the canonical prompt evaluation and regression-testing skill consumed by Prompt File Author and QA Tester.
- [skills/prompt-maintainer/SKILL.md](skills/prompt-maintainer/SKILL.md): Maintain and improve used GitHub Copilot *.prompt.md files after a run by comparing intended behavior vs implemented repo changes, then updating the same prompt file in place (tighten clarity/validation without changing intended functionality). Use when a prompt was already used and the user wants it improved for the next run. Composable with prompt-builder (create prompts), prompt-markdown-sanitizer (sanitize docs), and technical-overview-maintainer (update .github/Documentation/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md). Can run before or after sibling skills. Supports MCP tool interface.
- [skills/prompt-markdown-sanitizer/SKILL.md](skills/prompt-markdown-sanitizer/SKILL.md): Sanitize markdown prompt files (*.prompt.md) or engineering docs by fixing contradictions, missing inputs, wrong variable/tool usage, and unclear output/validation contracts. Use when a user asks to make a markdown prompt or doc reliable, unambiguous, and internally consistent without changing its intent. Composable with prompt-builder (create prompts), prompt-maintainer (post-run improvement), and technical-overview-maintainer (update .github/Documentation/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md). Can run before or after any sibling skill. Supports MCP tool interface.
- [skills/readme-blueprint-generator/SKILL.md](skills/readme-blueprint-generator/SKILL.md): Generate a comprehensive, developer-focused README.md for a repository by analyzing configuration-layer documentation and instructions.
- [skills/refactor-method-complexity-reduce/SKILL.md](skills/refactor-method-complexity-reduce/SKILL.md): Refactor given method `${input:methodName}` to reduce its cognitive complexity to `${input:complexityThreshold}` or below, by extracting helper methods.
- [skills/refactor-plan/SKILL.md](skills/refactor-plan/SKILL.md): Plan a multi-file refactor with proper sequencing and rollback steps
- [skills/refactor/SKILL.md](skills/refactor/SKILL.md): Surgical code refactoring to improve maintainability without changing behavior. Covers extracting functions, renaming variables, breaking down god functions, improving type safety, eliminating code smells, and applying design patterns. Less drastic than repo-rebuilder; use for gradual improvements.
- [skills/remember/SKILL.md](skills/remember/SKILL.md): Transforms lessons learned into domain-organized memory instructions (global or workspace). Syntax: `/remember [>domain [scope]] lesson clue` where scope is `global` (default), `user`, `workspace`, or `ws`.
- [skills/sdlc-backend-engineer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-backend-engineer/SKILL.md): API design, service implementation, data access patterns, and integration architecture. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-architect/SKILL.md): Threat modeling (STRIDE/DREAD), security architecture, Zero Trust design, compliance frameworks (OWASP, SOC 2, GDPR), and security requirements specification. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-cybersecurity-developer/SKILL.md): Secure coding practices, OWASP Top 10 and LLM Top 10 vulnerability remediation, SAST/DAST configuration, and security-focused testing. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-db-architect/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-db-architect/SKILL.md): Data modeling, schema design, normalization, partitioning, indexing strategy, and migration planning. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-db-developer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-db-developer/SKILL.md): Query optimization, stored procedures, migration scripts, performance tuning, and database operations. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-developer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-developer/SKILL.md): General-purpose software implementation with clean code principles, SOLID patterns, and production-grade quality. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-devops-engineer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-devops-engineer/SKILL.md): CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, containerization, monitoring, deployment strategies, and environment management. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-frontend-engineer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-frontend-engineer/SKILL.md): Senior frontend engineering — UI implementation, component architecture, state management, accessibility (WCAG 2.1 AA), and rendering performance. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-fullstack-engineer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-fullstack-engineer/SKILL.md): End-to-end feature implementation spanning frontend and backend. Bridges UI, API, and data layers. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-product-manager/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-product-manager/SKILL.md): Requirements analysis, user stories, GitHub issue creation, prioritization, roadmap planning, and hypothesis-driven development. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-qa-tester/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-qa-tester/SKILL.md): Test strategy, test case design, automation frameworks, quality gates, coverage analysis, and quality reporting. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-responsible-ai/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-responsible-ai/SKILL.md): Bias prevention, accessibility compliance, ethical AI development, privacy-by-design, and inclusive system design. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-scrum-master/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-scrum-master/SKILL.md): Agile coaching, sprint planning, ceremony facilitation, impediment removal, team velocity tracking, and continuous improvement. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-shared-memory/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-shared-memory/SKILL.md): Initialize, read, write, and maintain the .sdlc/ shared knowledge directory. Provides always-on centralized state management, automatic scaffolding, handoff management, task lifecycle, decision records, and contract protocols.
- [skills/sdlc-software-architect/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-software-architect/SKILL.md): System design, technology selection, component boundaries, scalability patterns, and architecture decision records. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-technical-writer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-technical-writer/SKILL.md): Developer documentation, API docs, architecture docs, tutorials, user guides, and ADRs. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/sdlc-ux-ui-designer/SKILL.md](skills/sdlc-ux-ui-designer/SKILL.md): Jobs-to-be-Done analysis, user journey mapping, flow specifications, design system guidance, and accessibility requirements. Works standalone or as part of an SDLC team.
- [skills/technical-overview-maintainer/SKILL.md](skills/technical-overview-maintainer/SKILL.md): Maintain a single-source-of-truth implementation document at .github/Documentation/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md. Use when the user requests an implementation or code change (feature, bug fix, refactor, config change, new endpoint) and the repo needs an up-to-date plan plus actual outcomes. Creates IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md if missing; updates it in the same run as the code changes. Documents request summary, files touched, implementation details, usage examples, and final status. Can run before or after prompt-maintainer (post-run prompt improvement) or prompt-markdown-sanitizer (sanitize docs). Composable via MCP tool interface.
- [skills/technical-path-indexer/SKILL.md](skills/technical-path-indexer/SKILL.md): Scan repository paths, extract routes and cross-file links, and emit deterministic technical path indexes. Use when an agent needs deterministic path intelligence before auditing, planning, or rendering a technical path document.
- [skills/technical-writing-diataxis/SKILL.md](skills/technical-writing-diataxis/SKILL.md): Diátaxis validation (tutorial/how-to/reference/explanation), docstring-driven reference generation, runnable-example enforcement, and plain-language scoring (Hemingway/Flesch) — the canonical documentation-structure skill consumed by Technical Writer.
- [skills/web-accessibility-audit/SKILL.md](skills/web-accessibility-audit/SKILL.md): Running WCAG 2.2 AA/AAA accessibility audits on web apps, combining automated tooling with manual checks, and producing a structured remediation report.
- [skills/web-coder/SKILL.md](skills/web-coder/SKILL.md): Expert 10x engineer with comprehensive knowledge of web development, internet protocols, and web standards. Use when working with HTML, CSS, JavaScript, web APIs, HTTP/HTTPS, web security, performance optimization, accessibility, or any web/internet concepts. Specializes in translating web terminology accurately and implementing modern web standards across frontend and backend development.
- [skills/web-design-reviewer/SKILL.md](skills/web-design-reviewer/SKILL.md): This skill enables visual inspection of websites running locally or remotely to identify and fix design issues. Triggers on requests like "review website design", "check the UI", "fix the layout", "find design problems". Detects issues with responsive design, accessibility, visual consistency, and layout breakage, then performs fixes at the source code level.
- [skills/web-design-system/SKILL.md](skills/web-design-system/SKILL.md): Building and maintaining production design systems — design tokens (color/spacing/typography/radius/shadow/elevation/motion), token tiers (global/alias/component), theming (light/dark, multi-brand), component APIs, accessibility primitives, Figma <-> code sync, versioning & migration.
- [skills/web-performance-budget/SKILL.md](skills/web-performance-budget/SKILL.md): Defining and enforcing Core Web Vitals performance budgets (LCP, INP, CLS, TTFB, TBT) and asset budgets across the build pipeline, with per-route enforcement in CI.
- [skills/what-context-needed/SKILL.md](skills/what-context-needed/SKILL.md): Determine which codebase files and context are required to answer a user question before performing reasoning or implementation.

## Instructions (instructions/*.instructions.md)

- [instructions/a11y.instructions.md](instructions/a11y.instructions.md): Guidance for creating more accessible code
- [instructions/agent-safety.instructions.md](instructions/agent-safety.instructions.md): Guidelines for building safe, governed AI agent systems. Apply when writing code that uses agent frameworks, tool-calling LLMs, or multi-agent orchestration to ensure proper safety boundaries, policy enforcement, and auditability.
- [instructions/agent-skills.instructions.md](instructions/agent-skills.instructions.md): Guidelines for creating high-quality Agent Skills for GitHub Copilot
- [instructions/agents.instructions.md](instructions/agents.instructions.md): Guidelines for creating custom agent files for GitHub Copilot
- [instructions/ai-prompt-engineering-safety-best-practices.instructions.md](instructions/ai-prompt-engineering-safety-best-practices.instructions.md): Comprehensive best practices for AI prompt engineering, safety frameworks, bias mitigation, and responsible AI usage for Copilot and LLMs.
- [instructions/aspnet-rest-apis.instructions.md](instructions/aspnet-rest-apis.instructions.md): Guidelines for building REST APIs with ASP.NET
- [instructions/astro.instructions.md](instructions/astro.instructions.md): Astro development standards and best practices for content-driven websites
- [instructions/asyncapi.instructions.md](instructions/asyncapi.instructions.md): AsyncAPI specification conventions for event-driven and message-based APIs — channels, messages, and schema reuse.
- [instructions/blazor.instructions.md](instructions/blazor.instructions.md): Blazor component and application patterns
- [instructions/bun.instructions.md](instructions/bun.instructions.md): Bun runtime conventions — Bun.serve streaming, native APIs, bundler/test-runner usage, and performance patterns.
- [instructions/clojure.instructions.md](instructions/clojure.instructions.md): Clojure-specific coding patterns, inline def usage, code block templates, and namespace handling for Clojure development.
- [instructions/cmake-vcpkg.instructions.md](instructions/cmake-vcpkg.instructions.md): C++ project configuration and package management
- [instructions/code-review-generic.instructions.md](instructions/code-review-generic.instructions.md): Generic code review instructions that can be customized for any project using GitHub Copilot
- [instructions/codexer.instructions.md](instructions/codexer.instructions.md): Advanced Python research assistant with Context 7 MCP integration, focusing on speed, reliability, and 10+ years of software development expertise
- [instructions/containerization-docker-best-practices.instructions.md](instructions/containerization-docker-best-practices.instructions.md): Comprehensive best practices for creating optimized, secure, and efficient Docker images and managing containers. Covers multi-stage builds, image layer optimization, security scanning, and runtime best practices.
- [instructions/context-engineering.instructions.md](instructions/context-engineering.instructions.md): Guidelines for structuring code and projects to maximize GitHub Copilot effectiveness through better context management
- [instructions/context7.instructions.md](instructions/context7.instructions.md): Use Context7 for authoritative external docs and API references when local context is insufficient
- [instructions/copilot-thought-logging.instructions.md](instructions/copilot-thought-logging.instructions.md): See process Copilot is following where you can edit this to reshape the interaction or save when follow up may be needed
- [instructions/cpp-language-service-tools.instructions.md](instructions/cpp-language-service-tools.instructions.md): You are an expert at using C++ language service tools (GetSymbolReferences_CppTools, GetSymbolInfo_CppTools, GetSymbolCallHierarchy_CppTools). Instructions for calling C++ Tools for Copilot. When working with C++ code, you have access to powerful language service tools that provide accurate, IntelliSense-powered analysis. **Always prefer these tools over manual code inspection, text search, or guessing.**
- [instructions/csharp-ja.instructions.md](instructions/csharp-ja.instructions.md): C# アプリケーション構築指針 by @tsubakimoto
- [instructions/csharp-ko.instructions.md](instructions/csharp-ko.instructions.md): C# 애플리케이션 개발을 위한 코드 작성 규칙 by @jgkim999
- [instructions/csharp-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/csharp-mcp-server.instructions.md): Instructions for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers using the C# SDK
- [instructions/csharp.instructions.md](instructions/csharp.instructions.md): Guidelines for building C# applications
- [instructions/dart-n-flutter.instructions.md](instructions/dart-n-flutter.instructions.md): Instructions for writing Dart and Flutter code following the official recommendations.
- [instructions/deno.instructions.md](instructions/deno.instructions.md): Deno runtime conventions — permissions model, standard library usage, and Deno Deploy edge patterns.
- [instructions/devops-core-principles.instructions.md](instructions/devops-core-principles.instructions.md): Foundational instructions covering core DevOps principles, culture (CALMS), and key metrics (DORA) to guide GitHub Copilot in understanding and promoting effective software delivery.
- [instructions/dotnet-architecture-good-practices.instructions.md](instructions/dotnet-architecture-good-practices.instructions.md): DDD and .NET architecture guidelines
- [instructions/dotnet-framework.instructions.md](instructions/dotnet-framework.instructions.md): Guidance for working with .NET Framework projects. Includes project structure, C# language version, NuGet management, and best practices.
- [instructions/dotnet-upgrade.instructions.md](instructions/dotnet-upgrade.instructions.md): Specialized agent for comprehensive .NET framework upgrades with progressive tracking and validation
- [instructions/dotnet-wpf.instructions.md](instructions/dotnet-wpf.instructions.md): .NET WPF component and application patterns
- [instructions/draw-io.instructions.md](instructions/draw-io.instructions.md): Use when creating, editing, or reviewing draw.io diagrams and mxGraph XML in .drawio, .drawio.svg, or .drawio.png files.
- [instructions/genaiscript.instructions.md](instructions/genaiscript.instructions.md): AI-powered script generation guidelines
- [instructions/gilfoyle-code-review.instructions.md](instructions/gilfoyle-code-review.instructions.md): Gilfoyle-style code review instructions that channel the sardonic technical supremacy of Silicon Valley's most arrogant systems architect.
- [instructions/git-conventional-commits.instructions.md](instructions/git-conventional-commits.instructions.md): Conventional Commits specification — commit message structure, types, breaking-change footers, and changelog/semver automation.
- [instructions/github-actions-workflows.instructions.md](instructions/github-actions-workflows.instructions.md): GitHub Actions workflow conventions — job structure, caching, secrets handling, and reusable workflows.
- [instructions/go-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/go-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices and patterns for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in Go using the official github.com/modelcontextprotocol/go-sdk package.
- [instructions/go.instructions.md](instructions/go.instructions.md): Instructions for writing Go code following idiomatic Go practices and community standards
- [instructions/html-css-style-color-guide.instructions.md](instructions/html-css-style-color-guide.instructions.md): Color usage guidelines and styling rules for HTML elements to ensure accessible, professional designs.
- [instructions/htmx.instructions.md](instructions/htmx.instructions.md): htmx conventions — hypermedia-driven UI, partial rendering, out-of-band swaps, and server-side patterns.
- [instructions/instructions.instructions.md](instructions/instructions.instructions.md): Guidelines for creating high-quality custom instruction files for GitHub Copilot
- [instructions/java-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/java-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices and patterns for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in Java using the official MCP Java SDK with reactive streams and Spring integration.
- [instructions/kotlin-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/kotlin-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices and patterns for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in Kotlin using the official io.modelcontextprotocol:kotlin-sdk library.
- [instructions/kotlin.instructions.md](instructions/kotlin.instructions.md): Idiomatic Kotlin conventions for JVM/Android development — coroutines, null-safety, and modern language features.
- [instructions/langchain-python.instructions.md](instructions/langchain-python.instructions.md): Instructions for using LangChain with Python
- [instructions/localization.instructions.md](instructions/localization.instructions.md): Guidelines for localizing markdown documents
- [instructions/makefile.instructions.md](instructions/makefile.instructions.md): Best practices for authoring GNU Make Makefiles
- [instructions/markdown-accessibility.instructions.md](instructions/markdown-accessibility.instructions.md): Markdown accessibility guidelines based on GitHub's 5 best practices for inclusive documentation
- [instructions/markdown-content-creation.instructions.md](instructions/markdown-content-creation.instructions.md): Markdown guidelines and content creation standards for blog posts
- [instructions/markdown-gfm.instructions.md](instructions/markdown-gfm.instructions.md): Markdown formatting for GitHub-flavored markdown (GFM) files
- [instructions/markdown.instructions.md](instructions/markdown.instructions.md): Markdown formatting aligned to the CommonMark specification (0.31.2)
- [instructions/memory-bank.instructions.md](instructions/memory-bank.instructions.md): Coding standards, domain knowledge, and preferences that AI should follow.
- [instructions/mongo-dba.instructions.md](instructions/mongo-dba.instructions.md): Instructions for customizing GitHub Copilot behavior for MONGODB DBA chat mode.
- [instructions/nestjs.instructions.md](instructions/nestjs.instructions.md): NestJS development standards and best practices for building scalable Node.js server-side applications
- [instructions/nextjs-tailwind.instructions.md](instructions/nextjs-tailwind.instructions.md): Next.js + Tailwind development standards and instructions
- [instructions/nextjs.instructions.md](instructions/nextjs.instructions.md): Best practices for building Next.js (App Router) apps with modern caching, tooling, and server/client boundaries (aligned with Next.js 16.1.1).
- [instructions/no-heredoc.instructions.md](instructions/no-heredoc.instructions.md): Prevents terminal heredoc file corruption in VS Code Copilot by enforcing use of file editing tools instead of shell redirections
- [instructions/nodejs-javascript-vitest.instructions.md](instructions/nodejs-javascript-vitest.instructions.md): Guidelines for writing Node.js and JavaScript code with Vitest testing
- [instructions/nuxt.instructions.md](instructions/nuxt.instructions.md): Nuxt 4 conventions — file-based routing, data fetching (useFetch/useAsyncData), server routes (Nitro), and SSR/hydration patterns.
- [instructions/object-calisthenics.instructions.md](instructions/object-calisthenics.instructions.md): Enforces Object Calisthenics principles for business domain code to ensure clean, maintainable, and robust code
- [instructions/oop-design-patterns.instructions.md](instructions/oop-design-patterns.instructions.md): Best practices for applying Object-Oriented Programming (OOP) design patterns, including Gang of Four (GoF) patterns and SOLID principles, to ensure clean, maintainable, and scalable code.
- [instructions/openapi-3-1.instructions.md](instructions/openapi-3-1.instructions.md): OpenAPI 3.1 specification conventions — JSON Schema alignment, versioning, and contract-first API design.
- [instructions/otel-otelcol.instructions.md](instructions/otel-otelcol.instructions.md): OpenTelemetry instrumentation and Collector (otelcol) conventions — traces, metrics, logs, and pipeline configuration.
- [instructions/performance-optimization.instructions.md](instructions/performance-optimization.instructions.md): The most comprehensive, practical, and engineer-authored performance optimization instructions for all languages, frameworks, and stacks. Covers frontend, backend, and database best practices with actionable guidance, scenario-based checklists, troubleshooting, and pro tips.
- [instructions/php-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/php-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices for building Model Context Protocol servers in PHP using the official PHP SDK with attribute-based discovery and multiple transport options
- [instructions/php-symfony.instructions.md](instructions/php-symfony.instructions.md): Symfony development standards aligned with official Symfony Best Practices
- [instructions/playwright-dotnet.instructions.md](instructions/playwright-dotnet.instructions.md): Playwright .NET test generation instructions
- [instructions/playwright-python.instructions.md](instructions/playwright-python.instructions.md): Playwright Python AI test generation instructions based on official documentation.
- [instructions/playwright-typescript.instructions.md](instructions/playwright-typescript.instructions.md): Playwright test generation instructions
- [instructions/postgres-tuning.instructions.md](instructions/postgres-tuning.instructions.md): PostgreSQL performance tuning — indexing strategy, query planning (EXPLAIN ANALYZE), connection pooling, and vacuum/autovacuum.
- [instructions/powershell-pester-5.instructions.md](instructions/powershell-pester-5.instructions.md): PowerShell Pester testing best practices based on Pester v5 conventions
- [instructions/powershell.instructions.md](instructions/powershell.instructions.md): PowerShell cmdlet and scripting best practices based on Microsoft guidelines
- [instructions/prompt.instructions.md](instructions/prompt.instructions.md): Guidelines for creating high-quality prompt files for GitHub Copilot
- [instructions/pulumi.instructions.md](instructions/pulumi.instructions.md): Pulumi conventions for infrastructure as code in general-purpose languages — stack/project structure, state, and secrets.
- [instructions/python-django.instructions.md](instructions/python-django.instructions.md): Django conventions for maintainable web apps — ORM usage, class-based views, forms, and security defaults.
- [instructions/python-fastapi.instructions.md](instructions/python-fastapi.instructions.md): FastAPI conventions for async Python APIs — dependency injection, Pydantic validation, and background tasks.
- [instructions/python-flask.instructions.md](instructions/python-flask.instructions.md): Flask conventions for lightweight Python web apps and APIs — blueprints, application factories, and extension usage.
- [instructions/python-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/python-mcp-server.instructions.md): Instructions for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers using the Python SDK
- [instructions/quarkus-mcp-server-sse.instructions.md](instructions/quarkus-mcp-server-sse.instructions.md): Quarkus and MCP Server with HTTP SSE transport development standards and instructions
- [instructions/quarkus.instructions.md](instructions/quarkus.instructions.md): Quarkus development standards and instructions
- [instructions/r.instructions.md](instructions/r.instructions.md): R language and document formats (R, Rmd, Quarto): coding standards and Copilot guidance for idiomatic, safe, and consistent code generation.
- [instructions/remix.instructions.md](instructions/remix.instructions.md): Remix (React Router v7 framework mode) conventions — loaders/actions, nested routing, and progressive enhancement.
- [instructions/ruby-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/ruby-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices and patterns for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in Ruby using the official MCP Ruby SDK gem.
- [instructions/ruby-on-rails.instructions.md](instructions/ruby-on-rails.instructions.md): Ruby on Rails coding conventions and guidelines
- [instructions/rust-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/rust-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices for building Model Context Protocol servers in Rust using the official rmcp SDK with async/await patterns
- [instructions/rust-web-axum.instructions.md](instructions/rust-web-axum.instructions.md): Axum web framework conventions for Rust — routing, extractors, middleware, SSE/streaming, and error handling.
- [instructions/rust.instructions.md](instructions/rust.instructions.md): Rust programming language coding conventions and best practices
- [instructions/scala2.instructions.md](instructions/scala2.instructions.md): Scala 2.12/2.13 programming language coding conventions and best practices following Databricks style guide for functional programming, type safety, and production code quality.
- [instructions/sdlc-shared-memory.instructions.md](instructions/sdlc-shared-memory.instructions.md): Standards for reading, writing, and maintaining `.sdlc/` shared state files across SDLC agents.
- [instructions/security-and-owasp.instructions.md](instructions/security-and-owasp.instructions.md): Comprehensive secure coding instructions for all languages and frameworks, based on OWASP Top 10 and industry best practices.
- [instructions/self-explanatory-code-commenting.instructions.md](instructions/self-explanatory-code-commenting.instructions.md): Guidelines for GitHub Copilot to write comments to achieve self-explanatory code with less comments. Examples are in JavaScript but it should work on any language that has comments.
- [instructions/shell.instructions.md](instructions/shell.instructions.md): Shell scripting best practices and conventions for bash, sh, zsh, and other shells
- [instructions/solid.instructions.md](instructions/solid.instructions.md): SolidJS conventions — fine-grained reactivity, signals, stores, and SolidStart patterns.
- [instructions/spec-driven-workflow-v1.instructions.md](instructions/spec-driven-workflow-v1.instructions.md): Specification-Driven Workflow v1 provides a structured approach to software development, ensuring that requirements are clearly defined, designs are meticulously planned, and implementations are thoroughly documented and validated.
- [instructions/springboot.instructions.md](instructions/springboot.instructions.md): Guidelines for building Spring Boot base applications
- [instructions/sql-sp-generation.instructions.md](instructions/sql-sp-generation.instructions.md): Guidelines for generating SQL statements and stored procedures
- [instructions/svelte.instructions.md](instructions/svelte.instructions.md): Svelte 5 and SvelteKit development standards and best practices for component-based user interfaces and full-stack applications
- [instructions/swift-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/swift-mcp-server.instructions.md): Best practices and patterns for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers in Swift using the official MCP Swift SDK package.
- [instructions/swift.instructions.md](instructions/swift.instructions.md): Modern Swift and SwiftUI conventions for iOS/macOS native apps — concurrency, state management, testing, and App Store readiness.
- [instructions/tailwind-v4-vite.instructions.md](instructions/tailwind-v4-vite.instructions.md): Tailwind CSS v4+ installation and configuration for Vite projects using the official @tailwindcss/vite plugin
- [instructions/taming-copilot.instructions.md](instructions/taming-copilot.instructions.md): Prevent Copilot from wreaking havoc across your codebase, keeping it under control.
- [instructions/tanstack-start-shadcn-tailwind.instructions.md](instructions/tanstack-start-shadcn-tailwind.instructions.md): Guidelines for building TanStack Start applications
- [instructions/task-implementation.instructions.md](instructions/task-implementation.instructions.md): Instructions for implementing task plans with progressive tracking and change record - Brought to you by microsoft/edge-ai
- [instructions/tasksync.instructions.md](instructions/tasksync.instructions.md): TaskSync V5 - Allows you to give the agent new instructions or feedback after completing a task using terminal while agent is running.
- [instructions/terraform.instructions.md](instructions/terraform.instructions.md): Terraform conventions for infrastructure as code — module structure, state management, and safe apply workflows.
- [instructions/typescript-mcp-server.instructions.md](instructions/typescript-mcp-server.instructions.md): Instructions for building Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers using the TypeScript SDK
- [instructions/update-code-from-shorthand.instructions.md](instructions/update-code-from-shorthand.instructions.md): Shorthand code will be in the file provided from the prompt or raw data in the prompt, and will be used to update the code file when the prompt has the text `UPDATE CODE FROM SHORTHAND`.
- [instructions/update-docs-on-code-change.instructions.md](instructions/update-docs-on-code-change.instructions.md): Automatically update README.md and documentation files when application code changes require documentation updates

## Workflows (workflows/*.workflow.md)

- [workflows/bug-triage.workflow.md](workflows/bug-triage.workflow.md): Triage a reported bug from intake to verified fix, with the minimum roles needed to (1) confirm the root cause, (2) produce a safe patch, (3) verify the fix, and (4) ship it. This is a **short, incident-style** workflow — it is not a substitute for the full SDLC sequential workflow.
- [workflows/docs-regen.workflow.md](workflows/docs-regen.workflow.md): Regenerate the project's user-facing documentation from the source of truth: the API contract, the repository path map, and the responsible-AI review. This is a **pipeline-style** workflow — Technical Writer is the writer, RepositoryPathAuditor and Responsible AI are quality gates.
- [workflows/sdlc-parallel.workflow.md](workflows/sdlc-parallel.workflow.md): Run SDLC agents concurrently when tasks have no dependencies. This workflow identifies which agents can run simultaneously and which must wait.
- [workflows/sdlc-sequential.workflow.md](workflows/sdlc-sequential.workflow.md): Run SDLC agents one at a time. The user controls the pace. Each agent picks up where the previous one left off using the `.sdlc/` shared state.
